content:
Done — the journal Stop hook from space-station is installed here and verified working.

**What it does:** on every Stop, `journal.py` captures the turn's conversation (user messages, tool calls with redacted file payloads, assistant text) into `.claude/journal/session-<id>.jsonl`. The "forces feedback" part: if the turn included file edits (Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit) and no screenshot was taken since, it **blocks the agent from stopping** and instructs it to pilot the XR headset to the work and capture a checkpoint screenshot, which the hook saves as a PNG under `.claude/journal/checkpoints/`. `SessionEnd` does a final sweep for late-flushed entries.

**Installed** (via the `iwsdk-adventures-init` skill, whose bundled `journal.py` is byte-identical to space-station's copy):
- `.claude/hooks/journal.py` (new, executable)
- `.claude/settings.json` — `Stop` + `SessionEnd` hooks added; your 46 permission entries untouched
- `.claude/journal/state/<session>.json` — seeded to the current transcript offset so this session's history (the whole shader audit) won't get dumped into the journal on first fire

**Tested** — 21/21 checks pass on a synthetic transcript exercising every decision path: blocks after edit-work with no screenshot, allows on the second stop in a chain (`stop_hook_active`, so no infinite loop), allows once a screenshot exists and persists the checkpoint PNG with its path recorded in the journal, allows chat-only turns, and handles the SessionEnd sweep with sane session metadata. I also ran it against this session's real ~1 MB transcript (output redirected to scratch): parsed cleanly, captured 52 tool calls, correctly decided "blocked — checkpoint required," no hook errors.

One caveat: Claude Code snapshots hooks at session start, so the hook won't fire in *this* session — it goes live on your next session (or after reviewing via `/hooks`). Also note I installed just the hook setup you asked for; the skill can additionally add the Adventures hub packaging script (`scripts/package-for-hub.mjs` + `npm run package:hub`) if you ever want to publish this project — say the word.
The hook fired live — that's the real end-to-end test happening right now. Let me complete the checkpoint flow: load the runtime tools and check for an active session.

content:
Checkpoint captured — the shot frames the wand-attached prism Tools panel (Draw/Line/Erase/Color/Brush/Pick, Undo/Redo) with the right controller in view, from inside the live XR session.

This also completes the live end-to-end test of the hook, and corrects one thing I said earlier: the hook **does** take effect in the current session — it just blocked my stop for real, delivered the checkpoint prompt, and I followed the full workflow it prescribes (session status → find subject via scene hierarchy → `xr_look_at` → `browser_screenshot`). When this turn ends, the hook will journal it and persist the screenshot to `.claude/journal/checkpoints/<session-id>/` automatically. The install is confirmed working in production, not just in the synthetic tests.
